modules.name: Introduction to Data
modules.description: Define terms associated with the field of data. Discuss foundational data concepts. Explain how databases arrange information. Identify different data types.

modules.name: Bias in Data
modules.description: Explain bias. Discuss cognitive and statistical bias as they relate to data analysis. Describe where and when bias can occur and the associated risks.

lessons.title: Introduction to Bias
topics: Where bias occurs in data work

modules.name: Gathering Data
modules.description: Identify file source types used in data storage. Explain databases in the context of data storage. Discuss online datasets and web scraping.

modules.name: Questioning Data
modules.description: Critically think about data. Differentiate between correlation and causation.

modules.name: Relational Data
modules.description: Read tabular data. Define data modeling. Explain data relationships and normalization. Read entity-relationship diagrams.

lessons.title: Data Modeling
topics: Conceptual vs logical vs physical models

lessons.title: Data Normalization
topics: First, second, and third normal forms

modules.name: Data Visualizations
modules.description: Identify visualization types. Explain pie chart limitations. Identify trends. Explain outliers and their impact.

lessons.title: Visualization Types
topics: Bar charts, line charts, scatter plots

modules.name: Statistics
modules.description: Apply statistical thinking. Identify abnormalities and trends using descriptive statistics. Explain probability and data uncertainty.

assessment.title: Summative Assessment: Cooking by the Book
items: Capstone project applying all course concepts
Questions weighted by topic: Intro to Data (10), Bias (5), Gathering (10), Questioning (5), Relational (15), Visualizations (10), Statistics (15)
